name: vatsal

metadata.name: vatsal
metadata.summary: Custom utility functions to reuse efficiently.

documentation:
# vatsal
Custom repository for many utility functions.

methods.name: vatsal.utils.File.__init__
documentation:
Initializes the File class object. Returns FileNotFoundError if file does not exist.

```
param file_path

Absolute path to file.
```

methods.name: vatsal.utils.File.read
documentation:
Reads the content of file. Returns a string or bytes.

```
param mode

Mode to open the file. Defaults to 'r'.

param encoding

Encoding mode. Defaults to 'utf-8'.
```

methods.name: vatsal.utils.File.write
documentation:
Writes the content of file. Returns a string or bytes.

```
param data

Data to write to the file.

param mode

Mode to open the file. Defaults to 'w'.

param encoding

Encoding mode. Defaults to 'utf-8'.
```

classes.name: vatsal.utils.ProgressPercentage

methods.name: vatsal.utils.ProgressPercentage.__init__
documentation:
Initialize ProgressPercentage object.
\:param filename: Name of the file

methods.name: vatsal.utils.ProgressPercentage.__call__
documentation:
Show progress of file upload. To simplify, assume this is hooked up to a single filename
\:param bytes_amount: Bytes uploaded
Callback function to show progress of file upload.

modules.name: vatsal.s3_operations
documentation:
This module contains a class for all S3 operations using boto3 client. It also contains a callback function to
show the progress of file upload.

Author: vatsal1306

classes.name: vatsal.s3_operations.S3

methods.name: vatsal.s3_operations.S3.__init__
documentation:
Initialize S3 client.
\:param region: AWS region (default: ap-south-1)

methods.name: vatsal.s3_operations.S3.create_bucket
documentation:
Create a new S3 bucket.
\:param bucket_name: Name of the bucket
\:return: True if bucket is created, else raise Exception

methods.name: vatsal.s3_operations.S3.get_all_buckets
documentation: Return a List of all S3 buckets.

methods.name: vatsal.s3_operations.S3.upload_file
documentation:
Upload a file to an S3 bucket.
\:param transfer_config: Transfer configuration for multipart upload
\:param file_name: Absolute path to file
\:param bucket: Bucket to upload to
\:param object_name: S3 object name. If not specified then file_name is used
\:param transfer_config: Transfer configuration for multipart upload
\:return: True if file is uploaded, else raise Exception

methods.name: vatsal.s3_operations.S3.download_file
documentation:
Download a file from S3 bucket.
\:param transfer_config: Transfer configuration for multipart download
\:param bucket: Bucket to download from
\:param object_name: S3 object name
\:param file_name: Absolute path to save the file
\:return: True if file is downloaded, else raise Exception

methods.name: vatsal.s3_operations.S3.check_bucket_exists
documentation:
Check if a bucket exists in S3.
\:param bucket_name: Name of the bucket
\:return: True if the bucket exists, else False

methods.name: vatsal.s3_operations.S3.delete_bucket
documentation:
Delete a bucket from S3.
\:param bucket_name: Name of the bucket
\:return: True if the bucket is deleted, else raise Exception

methods.name: vatsal.s3_operations.S3.delete_object
documentation:
Delete an object from S3.
\:param bucket_name: Name of the bucket
\:param object_name: Name of the object
\:return: True if the object is deleted, else raise Exception

methods.name: vatsal.s3_operations.S3.write_create_file
documentation:
Write data to a file in S3 or create an empty file.
\:param bucket_name: Name of the bucket
\:param object_name: Name of the object
\:param file_name: Name of the file to write or to create
\:param data: Data to write. If None, empty file is created
\:return: True if data is written, else raise Exception

methods.name: vatsal.s3_operations.S3.read_file
documentation:
Read a file from S3.
\:param file_name: Name of the file
\:param bucket_name: Name of the bucket
\:param object_name: Name of the object. if None, file_name is used to search in bucket
\:return: Data read from the file in string format.
Class for all S3 operations using boto3 client.
